title: Stephanopodium organense
intro: Stephanopodium organense, also known as "guanabanillo," is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Diapetiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Stephanopodium organense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Stephanopodium organense belongs to the family Dipterocarpaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This plant is a species of Stephanopodium, a genus of flowering plants native to South America. Stephanopodium organense is classified as a shrub or small tree, and it is a member of the order Malpighiales. The family Dipterocarpaceae is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Stephanopodium organense is native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically in the countries of Guyana and French Guiana.
